01_initial_request.input_data: single channel immunofluorescence images of human cells
01_initial_request.user_intent: developing an efficient and versatile pipeline for the automatic detection of centrioles

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Centriole detection requires high-quality immunofluorescence images of human cells. [Gap] No publicly available annotated dataset exists for evaluating automated centriole detection. [Decision] To achieve [reliable pipeline training], chose to use [Immunostaining] to perform [sample preparation] on [human tissue culture cells (RPE-1, U-2 OS)], expecting to obtain [immunostained cell samples with specific markers].

parameters.cell_lines: hTERT RPE-1, U-2 OS, hTERT RPE-1::tp53-/-
parameters.primary_antibodies: anti-Centrin-2, anti-Cep63, anti-Cep152, anti-CP110, anti-Pericentrin, anti-SAS6, anti-gamma-Tubulin

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cells immunostained for centriole/procentriole/PCM markers and DNA.

references.citation: Table 1, Additional file 1: Tables S1 and S2
references.relevance: Details the datasets and markers used.

references.citation: Wang et al., 2015; Wong et al., 2015
references.relevance: References for cell lines and Centrinone treatment.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Immunostained samples need to be imaged to generate data for the pipeline. [Gap] High resolution is needed to distinguish centrioles separated by ~300 nm. [Decision] To achieve [high-resolution image acquisition], chose to use [Zeiss Observer D1 wide-field microscope] to perform [z-stack imaging] on [immunostained coverslips], expecting to obtain [16-bit OME TIFF images].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Z-stacks of cells acquired with specified resolution and format.

references.citation: Materials and methods - Cell culture, staining and imaging
references.relevance: Describes the imaging setup and parameters.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Deep learning models require ground truth annotations for training. [Gap] Centriole positions need to be manually identified for the dataset. [Decision] To achieve [annotated training data], chose to use [Labelbox] to perform [manual annotation] on [max-projected single channel images], expecting to obtain [X-Y coordinates of foci and nucleus masks].

parameters.annotation_type: X-Y coordinates for foci, freehand segmentation for nuclei
parameters.output_format: .csv files for coordinates, masks for nuclei

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Annotated dataset with centriole positions and nucleus masks created.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] A robust detector is needed for sparse and minute foci in high resolution images. [Gap] Existing methods rely on hard-coded parameters or multi-channel input. [Decision] To achieve [accurate single-channel detection], chose to use [SpotNet] to perform [model training] on [512x512 image patches], expecting to obtain [trained weights for centriole/procentriole detection].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: SpotNet model trained to predict Gaussian probability maps for centriole foci.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Centriole numbers need to be scored per cell, requiring cell identification. [Gap] Centrioles must be linked to the containing cell. [Decision] To achieve [cell identification], chose to use [StarDist] to perform [nucleus segmentation] on [DNA channel images], expecting to obtain [nucleus masks for cell assignment].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Nuclei segmented and masks generated for cell scoring.

references.relevance: Describes the StarDist cell detection method.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Detected centrioles need to be assigned to cells and performance quantified. [Gap] A metric is needed to validate detection accuracy against ground truth. [Decision] To achieve [pipeline validation], chose to use [CenFind] to perform [scoring and evaluation] on [test set], expecting to obtain [F1-score metrics].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Average F1-score > 90% (0.908 ± 0.043) achieved on test set.

references.relevance: Reports the F1-score and evaluation method.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Performance should be compared against conventional computer-vision methods. [Gap] Need to establish a baseline for blob detection. [Decision] To achieve [benchmarking], chose to use [scikit-image] to perform [Laplacian of Gaussians detection] on [test set], expecting to obtain [baseline F1-score].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Average F1-score 0.810 ± 0.230, substantially less well than CenFind.

references.relevance: Theory behind LoG and blob detection.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Performance should be compared against existing deep learning methods. [Gap] Need to compare with prior CNN-based centrosome detection. [Decision] To achieve [benchmarking], chose to use [FociDetector] to perform [single-channel detection] on [test set], expecting to obtain [baseline F1-score].

references.relevance: Original pipeline using FociDetector for centrosomes.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: F1-score calculation using Hungarian algorithm on test set
03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. CenFind demonstrated robust and accurate detection of centrioles in single channel immunofluorescence images with an average F1-score > 90%.
